title: woof agent role configuration
description: Project-side declaration at .woof/agents.toml. Defines which dispatch wrapper (cld/cod), which model, and which extra flags are used per role. Woof builds the full subprocess invocation dynamically from these entries via 'woof dispatch claude --role X' / 'woof dispatch codex --role X' adapter commands; no hard-coded model IDs or shell aliases anywhere in the tool. If absent, defaults apply (in-session planner, cld -p story-executor with default model, cod critiquer with default model, all with empty MCP set).

roles.description: Roles invoked by woof. Any subset may be defined; missing roles fall back to defaults. orchestrator and gate-resolver are constrained to in-session execution (they share the user's interactive Claude Code session) and so use the in_session_role schema; the other three use dispatchable_role.

default_minutes.description: Per-subprocess timeout. Driver wraps cld/cod invocations with `timeout`. Hitting the timeout writes gate.md with triggered_by=['timeout'].

review_valve.description: Periodic review configuration (Stage 5 Check 9). Opens a review_gate every N completed stories AND once before epic close, surfacing accumulated minor critique findings.

every_n_stories.description: Open a review_gate after every N successful story commits.

end_of_epic.description: Open a review_gate after the last story completes, before epic close. Recommended on.

audit.description: Audit policy for committed artefacts written by woof dispatch. Controls conservative redaction and per-file size cap (Stage 5 Check 8, realised in S2).

enabled.description: Apply redaction and size cap before writing audit artefacts. Set to false to disable both filters (not recommended for production use).

max_bytes.description: Per-file size cap in bytes. Audit artefacts exceeding this are truncated at commit time; the full output overflows to the gitignored audit/raw/ path. Default 262144 (256 KB).

redact_patterns.description: Additional project-specific regex patterns to redact. Each pattern is matched against each output line; matching text is replaced with a [REDACTED:<reason>] marker. Built-in patterns (JWT, AWS keys, bearer tokens) are always applied when enabled=true.

harness.description: orchestrator and gate-resolver must share the user's interactive Claude Code session; they cannot dispatch to a subprocess.
Dispatch wrapper. cld for Claude subprocesses, cod for Codex subprocesses, in-session for roles that should share the user's interactive session even though they could be dispatched.

think.description: cld only. Toggles extended thinking via the wrapper's -t flag. Schema does not enforce the cld-only constraint; runtime ignores think when harness=cod.

mcp.description: MCP server names. Empty array (default) = no MCPs.

flags.description: Additional pass-through args appended after the wrapper's '--' separator.
